items.title: Trump offers federal worker buyouts in effort to shrink size of government
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>The White House on Tuesday began offering buyouts worth seven months of salary to all federal employees who opt to leave their jobs by Feb. 6 — part of President Donald Trump’s unprecedented overhaul of the U.S. government. #shorts #WhiteHouse #Trump #federalworkers #buyouts #POTUS</article>

items.title: LIVE: Blue Origin prepares for 29th flight of its New Shepard rocket (scrubbed)
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin prepares for the 29th flight of its New Shepard rocket, set to launch from Launch Site One in West Texas. The mission, known as NS-29, will simulate the moon's gravity for the first time and carry 30 payloads, nearly all of which focus on testing technologies crucial for lunar exploration.

Credit: Blue Origin

#blueorigin #newshepard #moon #space</article>

items.title: Serbia's prime minister resigns as anti-corruption protests grow
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Serbia’s populist Prime Minister Milos Vucevic said Tuesday he is stepping down following weeks of massive anti-corruption protests over the deadly collapse of a concrete canopy in November. The canopy collapse, which killed 15 people in the northern city of Novi Sad, has become a flashpoint reflecting wider discontent with the increasingly autocratic rule of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic. Read more: https://bit.ly/40RCM51

#serbia #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live as The Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists announces whether the time of the iconic “Doomsday Clock” will change. In January 2024, the Doomsday Clock was reset at 90 seconds to midnight, the closest to midnight it has ever been. The advocacy group started used a clock in 1947 to symbolize the potential and even likelihood of people doing something to end humanity.

Credit: The Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists
#doomsdayclock #doomsday #live</article>

items.title: What to know about the U.S. showdown with Colombia over deported migrants
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: South Korea President Yoon indicted on rebellion charges
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has become the country's first leader to be indicted, less than two weeks after he was the first to be detained.

The impeached, jailed president, who had been holed up in his presidential compound for weeks after issuing a shocking martial law decree last month, now faces rebellion charges that are punishable by the death penalty or life in prison. Read more: https://bit.ly/4avRnGl

items.content:
<article>As Australia Day was celebrated around the country on Sunday, there were also protests against what organizers called "Invasion Day", referring to the day Aboriginal people say they lost their land. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4awSky4

#australia #protest #news

items.title: UN peacekeeping force on situation in Lebanon ahead of ceasefire deadline
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A day before the deadline for the first phase of an agreement that ended the war between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group, a team from the Associated Press accompanied a UNIFIL peacekeeping force on a trip to border towns in south Lebanon. Read more here: https://bit.ly/40vm5ea

#lebanon #israel #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Thousands of Australians celebrate Australia Day
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Australia Day is being celebrated all over the country this Sunday. In Sydney, traditional ceremonies took place at dawn in various locations including Bondi beach and Barangaroo. The Sydney Opera House was also lit up with traditional paintings.

#australia #australiaday #news
